top_stories.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
top_stories.summary: Russian state-sponsored hackers are exploiting router vulnerabilities to silently harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens at massive scale across 18,000+ networks, representing a critical threat to token-based authentication security that IAM professionals must address.

top_stories.title: The death of standing privilege in the age of AI agents
top_stories.summary: AI agents are emerging as high-privilege non-human identities that expose critical gaps in traditional PAM frameworks, signaling a fundamental shift in how access governance and privileged access management must evolve.

top_stories.title: UNC6783 Hackers Use Fake Okta Pages in Corporate Breach Campaign
top_stories.summary: Threat actor UNC6783 is actively conducting corporate breaches by impersonating IT support staff and deploying fake Okta login pages to harvest credentials, representing a direct and serious threat to organizations relying on Okta for SSO and identity management.

top_stories.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
top_stories.summary: Article addresses the growing threat of credential-based attacks and the detection model shifts needed to counter them, directly relevant to IAM professionals managing identity security and authentication defenses.

top_stories.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
top_stories.summary: Specops outlines how identity-first Zero Trust architecture addresses credential theft and privilege escalation by enforcing least-privilege access, device trust, and lateral movement controls—directly relevant to IAM practitioners implementing zero trust strategies.

news.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
news.summary: Microsoft's massive patch update addressed 165 vulnerabilities with privilege elevation bugs comprising the majority, including two zero-days, directly impacting PAM and least-privilege access strategies that IAM professionals must prioritize for remediation.

news.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
news.summary: APT41's new zero-detection backdoor specifically targets cloud credential harvesting across AWS, Google, Azure, and Alibaba, making it a critical threat for IAM teams managing cloud identity and access controls.

news.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
news.summary: Fancy Bear (APT28) continues targeted attacks globally, with security experts emphasizing zero trust architecture as a non-negotiable defense, making this directly relevant to IAM professionals implementing ZTA strategies.

news.title: Over 100 Chrome Web Store extensions steal user accounts, data
news.summary: Over 100 malicious Chrome extensions are actively stealing Google OAuth2 Bearer tokens, posing a direct threat to IAM professionals who rely on OAuth-based authentication flows and need to assess browser extension risks in their zero-trust and endpoint security strategies.

news.title: Critical flaw in wolfSSL library enables forged certificate use
news.summary: A critical flaw in the wolfSSL library allows forged certificate acceptance due to improper ECDSA signature verification, which is relevant to IAM professionals as certificate-based authentication and PKI underpin many identity and access systems.

news.title: FBI takedown of W3LL phishing service leads to developer arrest
news.summary: The FBI dismantled the W3LL phishing-as-a-service platform known for sophisticated MFA bypass capabilities and adversary-in-the-middle attacks targeting Microsoft 365 accounts, representing a significant takedown of infrastructure that directly undermined enterprise identity security controls.

news.title: Product showcase: Stop secrets from leaking through AI coding tools with GitGuardian
news.summary: GitGuardian's ggshield now includes AI coding tool hooks to prevent secrets (API keys, credentials) from leaking through tools like Copilot and Cursor, addressing a growing secrets management concern relevant to IAM teams responsible for credential security.

news.title: Microsoft ends desktop detour for sensitivity labels in Office web apps
news.summary: Microsoft now supports user-defined permissions for sensitivity labels directly in Office web apps (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), closing a capability gap with desktop clients and giving IAM/data governance teams more consistent policy enforcement across access channels.

news.title: W3LL phishing service sold for $500 dismantled by the FBI
news.summary: The FBI dismantled the W3LL phishing-as-a-service kit used to clone trusted login portals and harvest credentials at scale, directly threatening authentication integrity and underscoring the need for phishing-resistant MFA adoption.

news.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
news.summary: The takedown of the W3LL phishing-as-a-service platform is significant for IAM professionals as it specifically targeted Microsoft 365 account credentials and was engineered to bypass MFA protections, representing a major threat to enterprise identity security.

news.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
news.summary: Google's Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) is now GA in Chrome 146 for Windows, cryptographically binding browser sessions to devices to prevent session token theft—directly addressing a major authentication bypass threat that IAM and security teams face when cookie/session hijacking circumvents MFA.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" uses reverse-proxy techniques to relay real login pages and intercept live MFA tokens in real-time, directly undermining MFA as a security control and posing a significant threat to IAM defenses relying on traditional multi-factor authentication.

news.title: Show HN: Prmana – OIDC SSH Login for Linux with DPoP (Rust, Apache 2.0)
news.summary: Prmana is a new open-source Rust tool that replaces static SSH keys with short-lived OIDC tokens using DPoP (RFC 9449) proof-of-possession validated via PAM, offering IAM professionals a modern approach to eliminating long-lived SSH credentials in Linux environments.

news.title: [Sponsor] WorkOS FGA: The Authorization Layer for AI Agents
news.summary: WorkOS launches Fine-Grained Authorization (FGA) product targeting AI agent authorization, relevant to IAM professionals evaluating access control solutions for agentic AI workflows.

news.title: Sapiom CEO: Software is becoming the ‘customer' of the Internet
news.summary: Sapiom's CEO argues that AI agents are becoming primary internet users, raising emerging questions about non-human identity and authentication models that IAM professionals will need to address.

news.title: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities
news.summary: AppViewX CEO discusses the convergence of machine and AI agent identity governance, drawing on CyberArk experience to address visibility and control challenges that are increasingly critical for IAM teams managing non-human identities.

news.title: Google Chrome Update Disrupts Infostealer Cookie Theft
news.summary: Google's DBSC feature in Chrome 146 binds session cookies to hardware keys, directly countering session hijacking attacks that bypass MFA and threaten enterprise SSO implementations.

news.title: To counter cookie theft, Chrome ships device-bound session credentials
news.summary: Chrome is shipping device-bound session credentials to cryptographically tie authentication cookies to specific devices, directly countering session hijacking attacks that bypass MFA and other IAM controls.

news.title: Google Chrome 146 Adds Device Bound Session Credentials to Stop Session Cookie Theft on Windows
news.summary: Chrome 146 introduces Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) on Windows, cryptographically binding session cookies to the device to prevent session hijacking attacks—directly relevant to IAM professionals managing authentication security and mitigating token/cookie theft threats.

news.title: Google Chrome adds infostealer protection against session cookie theft
news.summary: Google Chrome 146's Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) cryptographically binds session cookies to specific devices on Windows, directly countering session hijacking attacks that bypass MFA and authentication controls—a significant development for IAM professionals managing browser-based access security.

news.title: 'Several dozen' high-value corporations hit by new extortion crew in helpdesk phishing spree
news.summary: A new extortion group is compromising high-value corporations via helpdesk social engineering and phishing attacks, directly targeting identity verification weaknesses that IAM professionals are responsible for defending against.

news.title: Investigating Storm-2755: “Payroll pirate” attacks targeting Canadian employees
news.summary: Microsoft DART researchers uncovered Storm-2755, a threat actor targeting Canadian employee accounts to hijack identity profiles and redirect payroll — a direct identity compromise campaign relevant to IAM professionals managing workforce authentication and account security controls.

news.title: When attackers already have the keys, MFA is just another door to open
news.summary: Article examines how stolen credentials undermine MFA effectiveness and promotes wearable biometric authentication as a solution to phishing relay attacks and MFA bypass techniques, relevant to IAM professionals evaluating authentication security gaps.

news.title: Keycloak 26.6 bringt Zero-Downtime-Updates und Workflows
news.summary: Keycloak 26.6 promotes five features to production status, including Federated Client Authentication and zero-downtime updates, offering IAM teams running open-source identity infrastructure meaningful stability and operational improvements.
